title: Add Questions flow with auto-detect, layout/CTA fixes, UI copy updates, logo + export rendering fixes

requirements.summary: Prevent bottom action buttons from overflowing/colliding with fixed UI across key screens and viewports.
acceptanceCriteria: On common mobile viewport sizes (small/medium) and desktop, no page shows action buttons cut off below the fold due to fixed positioning or overflow issues.
Floating buttons (e.g., AI FAB) do not cover critical bottom CTAs; spacing/padding accounts for them when needed.
No horizontal scrolling is introduced as a side-effect of the fix.

file_operations.description: Introduce a reusable, safe-area-aware bottom spacing pattern for pages that have fixed/floating UI (e.g., use a CSS utility/class for consistent bottom padding and print-safe behavior) and ensure no new horizontal overflow is introduced.

Adjust FAB positioning and/or expose a predictable bottom inset so it does not cover critical bottom CTAs; ensure it respects safe-area insets and cooperates with page bottom padding. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Apply the shared page layout/bottom-padding treatment so the screen content and any bottom actions remain reachable and are not obscured by floating UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Apply the shared page layout/bottom-padding treatment so bottom actions and inputs remain visible on mobile with the FAB present. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Apply the shared page layout/bottom-padding treatment to prevent bottom CTAs (e.g., Continue button area) from being cut off on mobile/desktop. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Apply the shared page layout/bottom-padding treatment so any editor bottom actions/tooling remain reachable and are not hidden behind mobile browser chrome/floating elements. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Apply the shared page layout/bottom-padding treatment so export/print actions remain reachable and do not overflow on mobile/desktop. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Apply the shared page layout/bottom-padding treatment so filters and any bottom actions are not obscured by floating UI on mobile. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add top-of-screen "Add Questions" buttons on Home and Question Bank that open a full-screen Add Questions page.
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen shows a clearly visible, small "Add Questions" button near the top area (not only a floating button).
Question Bank screen shows a clearly visible, small "Add Questions" button near the top area.
Tapping either button navigates to the Add Questions page, which renders as a full-screen page (not a small dialog).

file_operations.description: Add a small "Add Questions" button near the top area, styled consistently with existing home quick-action UI, and navigate to the full-screen Add Questions route. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add a small "Add Questions" button near the top area and wire it to navigate to the full-screen Add Questions route. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Register a new route for the full-screen Add Questions page and ensure it is reachable via navigation from Home and Question Bank.

requirements.summary: Create a full-screen Add Questions flow for manual entry/paste into the personal Question Bank, categorized by Board and Standard.
acceptanceCriteria: The Add Questions flow allows entering or pasting one or multiple questions without first choosing the question type.
Before saving to the personal bank, the flow captures Board and Standard (either user-selected or confirmed) and stores them on the created questions.
Newly added questions appear under the correct Question Bank board/standard listing in "My Questions" after saving.
The flow does not incorrectly route questions into a paper section/heading when the intent is Question Bank storage.

file_operations.description: Implement the full-screen Add Questions page UI: manual entry/paste of one or multiple questions, Board + Standard capture, and saving into the personal Question Bank (not into paper sections/headings). Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Card, Select, Textarea, Alert) for consistent UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add/extend store capabilities needed by the Add Questions flow to create multiple personal questions with board/standard metadata and keep Question Bank listings in sync (using the existing persistence mechanism already used by the app).

Ensure the personal Question Bank board/standard listing reflects newly added questions immediately after saving (and remains correct after refresh via the existing persisted store). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the new Add Questions page route into the router so it renders as a full-screen page.

requirements.summary: Auto-detect question type immediately on paste/add, require per-question confirmation or per-question selection if rejected, and save confirmed types.
acceptanceCriteria: As soon as a question is added/pasted, detection runs automatically without an extra button click.
For each question, the UI shows the detected question type and asks the user to confirm or change it.
If the user indicates the detection is wrong, the UI forces selecting a type for that question before saving.
Saved questions reflect the confirmed type in the Question Bank filter (e.g., visible under the selected type).

file_operations.description: Add a lightweight, deterministic question-type detection helper (heuristics-based) that can run instantly on newly added/pasted question text and returns a best-guess QuestionType for UI confirmation.

Run type detection immediately when a question is added/pasted; show per-question confirmation UI and enforce explicit selection when detection is rejected; block saving until each question has a confirmed type. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Select, Dialog/Alert, Button) for confirmation/changing types. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure the question type filter correctly reflects newly saved question types (and that labels use the taxonomy mapping). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Update specific per-screen help/empty-state texts exactly as provided, without affecting other screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Question Bank screen shows exactly: "Select a board and standard to browse available questions. Add questions to sections to build your question paper." in its designated information/help area.
AI Assistant screen shows exactly: "Create questions based on selected topics, standards, and preferences." in its designated description/help area.
Board+Standard screen shows exactly: "Find questions using question type" in its designated help/description area.

When there are zero questions in the Board+Standard screen list, the empty state text is exactly:
"No questions added yet.
Start by selecting add questions ."
No unrelated labels or descriptions on other screens are overwritten with these strings.

file_operations.description: Replace only the Question Bank screen's designated info/help text with exactly: "Select a board and standard to browse available questions. Add questions to sections to build your question paper." Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace only the AI Assistant screen's designated description text with exactly: "Create questions based on selected topics, standards, and preferences." Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update only this screen's designated help/description text to exactly: "Find questions using question type" and replace the empty-state label "empty add questions" with exactly the two-line message:
"No questions added yet.
Start by selecting add questions ." Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fix Teacher Profile logo upload: enable selection, persist in profile storage, and render in editor header and export/print preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile screen’s "School Logo" file input is enabled and accepts image files.
After selecting a logo and saving, the logo persists across refresh (stored with the profile state mechanism already used by the app).
The logo is visible in the paper editor header area (where institute name/branding is displayed) and in export/print preview output.
If no logo is set, UI remains clean (no broken image icon).

file_operations.description: Enable the "School Logo" file input, accept image files, preview the chosen logo, and store it into the existing profile state on Save (using the app's current profile persistence mechanism). Use shadcn-ui Input/Button patterns already used on the page. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Extend the stored profile shape/persistence to include a saved school logo value (e.g., a safe serializable representation) and ensure it persists across refresh using the existing local persistence approach.

Render the persisted logo in the paper header/branding area used by both editor surfaces and the export/print preview; ensure no broken image is shown when absent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure export/print preview reflects the stored logo in the rendered paper output and that print-hidden UI does not affect the printable header. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Make institute name editable in the paper editor and ensure clean logo+name layout in editor and exported/printed output.
acceptanceCriteria: Paper editor includes a clear way to edit institute name for the current paper (overriding profile defaults where applicable).
Logo and institute name render without overlap/cropping on small and large screens.
Export/print preview uses the edited institute name and renders it aligned with the logo.

file_operations.description: Add an editor control to modify the institute name for the current paper (overriding profile defaults where applicable) and save it into the paper state; ensure the control does not break mobile layout. Use shadcn-ui Input/Button where appropriate. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Persist a per-paper institute name override (separate from profile defaults) and ensure it is saved/restored via the existing paper persistence mechanism.

Update the header/branding rendering to use the paper's institute name override when present; ensure the logo and institute name are aligned cleanly with responsive behavior and print compatibility. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure exported/printed output uses the edited institute name (paper override) and that header layout remains aligned with the logo in the printable view. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fix export/print rendering for images and tables so they fit page margins, preserve aspect ratios, and avoid clipping.
acceptanceCriteria: In export/print preview, rich content images and attachment images never overflow outside the printable area; they scale down to fit width and/or height constraints.
Printed/PDF output shows the same images/tables (no missing elements) and they are not clipped by page bounds.
Tables render with visible borders and do not break layout on narrow screens; print output remains readable.
Aspect ratio of images is preserved (no distortion).

file_operations.description: Strengthen print and preview CSS rules for the paper content area: constrain images to printable width while preserving aspect ratio, prevent clipping, and ensure tables render with readable borders and non-clipped layout in preview and print.

Ensure rendered question content applies print-safe classes/styling for images (attachments and rich-cell images) and tables so they resize within margins without distortion and avoid clipping. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adjust rich-cell rendering output (as used by preview/print) to apply consistent constraints for embedded images/tables to prevent overflow/clipping in the final print/PDF output. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure the preview container does not clip wide content (especially tables) and matches print constraints so preview and print/PDF are consistent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
